# Page 1^page=1
1?T R:&AT S:&&T wAl& RaAN@&S Review edits and craft a commit transaction $ git status aists all new or modiﬁed ﬁles to be committed $ git add [file] Snapshots the ﬁle in preparation for versioning $ git reset [file] Unstages the ﬁle, but preserve its contents $ git diff Shows ﬁle diﬀerences not yet staged $ git diff --staged Shows ﬁle diﬀerences between staging and the last ﬁle version $ git commit -m "[descriptive message]" Records ﬁle snapshots permanently in version history RON.f@UR& TOOrfN@ Ronﬁgure user information for all local repositories $ git config --global user.name "[name]" Sets the name you want attached to your commit transactions $ git config --global user.email "[email address]" Sets the email you want attached to your commit transactions $ git config --global color.ui auto &nables helpful colorization of command line output RR&AT& R&POSfTORf&S Start a new repository or obtain one from an existing URa $ git init [project-name] Rreates a new local repository with the speciﬁed name $ git clone [url] "ownloads a project and its entire version history @ROUP RaAN@&S ﬁame a series of commits and combine completed eﬀorts $ git branch aists all local branches in the current repository $ git branch [branch-name] Rreates a new branch $ git checkout [branch-name] Switches to the speciﬁed branch and updates the working directory $ git merge [branch] Rombines the speciﬁed branch’s history into the current branch $ git branch -d [branch-name] "eletes the speciﬁed branch 1it is the open source distributed version control system that facilitates 1it:ub activities on your laptop or desktop. This cheat sheet summarizes commonly used 1it command line instructions for quick reference. fNSTArr @fT 1it:ub provides desktop clients that include a graphical user interface for the most common repository actions and an automati- cally updating command line edition of 1it for advanced scenarios. @itaub for Windows https://windows.github.com @itaub for wac https://mac.github.com 1it distributions for ainux and PﬀS?X systems are available on the oﬃcial 1it SR' web site. @it for All Platforms http://git-scm.com V 1.1.1

# Page 2^page=2
training@github.com training.github.com aearn more about using 1it:ub and 1it. &mail the Training Team or visit our web site for learning event schedules and private class availability. 1?T R:&AT S:&&T SYNRaRONfZ& RaAN@&S Register a repository bookmark and exchange version history $ git fetch [bookmark] "ownloads all history from the repository bookmark $ git merge [bookmark]/[branch] Rombines bookmark’s branch into current local branch $ git push [alias] [branch] Uploads all local branch commits to 1it:ub $ git pull "ownloads bookmark history and incorporates changes R&.ARTOR .fr&NAw&S Relocate and remove versioned ﬁles $ git rm [file] "eletes the ﬁle from the working directory and stages the deletion $ git rm --cached [file] Removes the ﬁle from version control but preserves the ﬁle locally $ git mv [file-original] [file-renamed] Rhanges the ﬁle name and prepares it for commit SAV& .RA@w&NTS Shelve and restore incomplete changes $ git stash Temporarily stores all modiﬁed tracked ﬁles $ git stash list aists all stashed changesets $ git stash pop Restores the most recently stashed ﬁles $ git stash drop "iscards the most recently stashed changeset R&YO ROwwfTS &rase mistakes and craft replacement history $ git reset [commit] Undoes all commits after [commit], preserving changes locally $ git reset --hard [commit] "iscards all history and changes back to the speciﬁed commit R&Vf&W afSTORY Lrowse and inspect the evolution of project ﬁles $ git log aists version history for the current branch $ git log --follow [file] aists version history for a ﬁle, including renames $ git diff [first-branch]...[second-branch] Shows content diﬀerences between two branches $ git show [commit] ﬀutputs metadata and content changes of the speciﬁed commit SUPPR&SS TRARlfN@ &xclude temporary ﬁles and paths $ git ls-files --other --ignored --exclude-standard aists all ignored ﬁles in this project *.log build/ temp-* A text ﬁle named .gitignore suppresses accidental versioning of ﬁles and paths matching the speciﬁed patterns
